_about: Motor and power configuration for the sitl_default test vehicle (3 kg generic QuadPlane). Tuned to match the canonical ArduPilot SITL QuadPlane plant the official quadplane.parm PIDs target: minimal motor lag, no artificial rotational damping (the closed-loop rate-PID + small Ixx provides natural damping).

_doc.pwm_min: Minimum PWM signal sent to motors in microseconds. Matches Q_M_PWM_MIN in params.parm (1000). ArduPilot SITL default.
_doc.pwm_max: Maximum PWM signal sent to motors in microseconds. Matches Q_M_PWM_MAX in params.parm (2000). ArduPilot SITL default.
_doc.k_thrust: Lift motor thrust coefficient in Newtons. T = k_thrust * throttle^2. Derived: k_thrust = (mass_kg * 9.81 / 4) / Q_M_THST_HOVER^2 = (3 * 9.81 / 4) / 0.35^2 = 7.3575 / 0.1225 = 60.1 N. This ensures the vehicle hovers at exactly Q_M_THST_HOVER=0.35 throttle, which is ArduPilot's default hover point.
_doc.k_torque: Lift motor reaction torque coefficient in N·m. Q = k_torque * throttle^2. Used for yaw control via CW/CCW motor imbalance. Conservative value — increase if yaw authority is too weak.
_doc.k_thrust_pusher: Pusher motor thrust coefficient in Newtons. Provides forward thrust for fixed-wing cruise. 8 N at full throttle is sufficient for a 3 kg vehicle to maintain level flight above stall speed.
_doc.motor_time_constant_s: First-order lag time constant for motor thrust, in seconds. 0.05 s is realistic for small brushless motors and prevents instant step torques at liftoff (which would otherwise drive AP's rate-I integrator to saturation in one tick).
_doc.rotational_damping: Rotational damping coefficient in N·m/(rad/s). 1.5 adds damping that real multirotors get from rotor-disk asymmetry and body aero at hover — without it, the angle→rate cascade with real PIDs becomes two stacked integrators and oscillates.
_doc.drag_coeff: Linear body drag coefficient in N/(m/s). Opposes translational velocity. Modest value for a small VTOL airframe in low-speed regime.
